article_title: Over 700 Fossils from China's Jiangchuan Biota Push the Origin of Complex Animals Back into the Ediacaran

content_preview.title: Over 700 Fossils from China's Jiangchuan Biota Push the Origin of Complex Animals Back into the Ediacaran
content_preview.summary: A fossil assemblage preserved as carbonaceous films in Yunnan Province reveals more than 700 specimens of complex animals, including the oldest known deuterostomes, dating to 554-539 million years ago and closing a major gap between the Ediacaran period and the Cambrian explosion.
content_preview.body_excerpt: An international team of paleontologists has described a trove of more than 700 fossils from southwestern China that fills one of the most significant gaps in the record of early animal life, according to a [study published in Science](https://www.science.org/doi/10.1126/science.adu2291) on April 3, 2026. The site, known as the Jiangchuan biota in Yunnan Province, preserves organisms dating to between 554 and 539 million years ago, placing them squarely in the late Ediacaran period, at least fou...

recommendations: 1 source(s) blocked bot access — sources likely valid but not archivable automatically

editor_notes.content_quality: Excellent paleontology article making complex evolutionary biology accessible. Good structure and clear significance framing.
editor_notes.source_verification: Oxford press release: verified — 700+ fossils, Jiangchuan biota, carbonaceous films, oldest deuterostomes, researcher names and quotes confirmed. EurekAlert: verified — transitional fauna, Science publication, Yunnan University/Oxford collaboration confirmed. Science.org: 403 paywall, DOI confirmed via EurekAlert.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Core claims verified. Minor note: EurekAlert mentions 575-539 Ma range vs article's 554-539 Ma — may reflect different dating approaches for different layers. Not blocking.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and accessible. No sensationalism despite the significance of the discovery.
editor_notes.originality: Fresh topic — no prior Jiangchuan biota coverage.
concerns: Minor date range discrepancy (554 vs 575 Ma lower bound) between article and EurekAlert
